description: kernelCTF-latest (lts-6.12.91 hunt-mode kernel): KASAN-replay over the boot-only dmesg log produced by run_qemu_latest.sh. No exploit ran; expected verdict is 'inconclusive' (no_crash ≠ safe), confirming that the boot smoke is healthy and the dispatch wiring is intact. When a real fuzz pass eventually produces a new dmesg with a KASAN report, this same candidate will flip to 'confirmed'.

Positive control: same dispatch wiring routed against the Phase-0.4 historical 6.1.72 dmesg (NF_TABLES=y, CVE-2024-1086 exploit fires). Expected: 'confirmed' KASAN UAF. Proves the kernelCTF-latest candidate shape is dispatch-identical to the historical one and only the underlying dmesg differs.
